meta.json_run_notes: Clinical execution of CA-1102.5. Metadata and integrity checks performed to ensure zero parent-slug leakage and high source fidelity.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Any rule, regulation, or policy preventing an employee from disclosing information, or retaliating against an employee for disclosing information to a government or law enforcement agency.

statute_of_limitations.trigger: The date of the retaliatory act

burden_of_proof.employer_defense_details: Employer must demonstrate by clear and convincing evidence that the action would have occurred for legitimate, independent reasons even if the employee had not engaged in protected activity.
burden_of_proof.burden_of_proof_details: Codified framework under Labor Code § 1102.6 requires employee to show protected activity was a contributing factor; burden then shifts to employer for 'clear and convincing' rebuttal.

attached_citations: Lawson v. PPG Architectural Finishes, Inc. || clarifies burden-shifting standard || https://scholar.google.com/scholar_case?case=17614050212048999805 || Google Scholar || high

records._review_notes: Statute provides broad protection for employees reporting violations of state or federal statutes. Reviewer should note that 2024 amendments specifically codified the Lawson burden-shifting standard into the section's implementation logic via cross-reference to 1102.6.

error_details: CA-1102.5: attached_citations used scholar.google.com URL per template fallback as high-tier official case URL for Lawson was not immediately verifiable via courtlistener/casetext deep-links.
